finding_results.notes: Replaced four consecutive Agnes monologue blocks (original lines 147-159) with shorter volleys across lines 147-167. Carmen now has six active dialogue turns: a procedural retreat cut off by Agnes (line 149: 'That's my job. I check on every resident on this —'), a self-protective restart with concrete stakes (line 153: 'You don't know what I'm risking... I could lose my license. My kid depends on this job.'), a flat challenge to the nature of the work (line 157: 'What work?... Harry running pills out of a nursing home is not —'), and a moral question Agnes declines to answer (line 165: 'And that makes it okay?'). Each Carmen move materially redirects Agnes's next line: the procedural retreat forces Agnes to specify which doors Carmen watches; the license concern gets acknowledged before Agnes pivots to complicity; the 'What work?' challenge makes Agnes cite concrete pharmaceutical evidence (hydroxychloroquine interaction, three notebooks, NDC codes) instead of delivering moral diagnosis; the 'okay?' question makes Agnes drop argument and move to the actionable ask. Meeting invitation now lands as its own beat (line 167) after the fought-through exchange, not inside a diagnosis block. Cut thesis-speech ('You are managing yourself'), quotable landing lines ('stop making your uncertainty the event'), narrator admiration ('paused for exactly the interval required to separate an acknowledgment from a dismissal'), and extended moral diagnosis ('The primary problem is that people outside this building cannot afford...'). Agnes remains low-contraction, clinically exact, blunt. Carmen's voice preserved: high contraction, retreats into procedure, trails off when she can't finish honestly, warmth drops under pressure. Agnes's notebooks setup (payoff chapter 17) preserved and sharpened with 'Three notebooks' and NDC-code specifics.

finding_results.notes: Converted the cited Agnes-POV shorthand beats into full sentences or integrated them into surrounding syntax, and cleaned adjacent narration fragments so the chapter now conforms to the constitution's anti-elliptical-clipping rule without flattening her compressed voice.

Retained Agnes's Walgreens tenure as an early career anchor, then shifted the persuasion scene away from repeated pharmacist/forty-two-years assertions and toward operational proof: buyer-medication screening, dangerous-sale veto power, notebook controls, and pill-identification expertise.

pass_summaries.summary: Chapter 8 was revised in place to align with late-September continuity, sharpen Agnes's POV into a more clinical and distinct narrative texture, and resolve the roommate-name continuity break.

Single finding addressed: the Agnes/Carmen confrontation (lines 145-169) was reworked from composed-diagnosis monologue structure into shorter volleys with genuine leverage shifts. Agnes's four stacked mini-speeches were broken into exchanges where Carmen pushes back with procedural retreat, self-protective restart, challenge to the work's legitimacy, and moral question — each redirecting Agnes's next line. Agnes now presses with concrete operational facts (hydroxychloroquine interaction, three notebooks, NDC codes) rather than psychological diagnosis of Carmen. Meeting invitation lands after the exchange as its own beat. Quotable thesis-speech and narrator admiration removed. Agnes's low-contraction clinical register and Carmen's high-contraction pressured texture both preserved. No changes outside the flagged passage; heading contract, chapter ending, notebook setup, pill-identification scene, and all continuity details intact.

Revised chapter 8's cited clipped beats into grammatical Agnes-voice prose and replaced the late credential refrain with concrete pharmaceutical operations that show Agnes's authority instead of reasserting it.
